stimulus.content: In December 1773, colonists in Boston were angry about a tax on tea. A group of men dressed as Mohawk Indians boarded British ships at night. They dumped 342 chests of tea into Boston Harbor. This event became known as the Boston Tea Party.

stem: What were the colonists protesting during the Boston Tea Party?

choices.text: A ban on fishing in the harbor

answer_explanation: The passage states colonists were angry about a tax on tea, which led them to dump tea into Boston Harbor. The other choices are not mentioned in the passage.
